semanticCore: Poverty, flattery, wiping out, smooth stone, or lack
quranicRelevance: Killing children due to poverty (Imlaq - 'min imlaqin', 'khashyata imlaqin').

keyDerivations.meaning: Poverty, destitution, financial depletion, lack of resources, or starvation

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلَادَكُمْ خَشْيَةَ إِمْلَاقٍ ۖ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُهُمْ وَإِيَّاكُمْ (17:31): And do not kill your children out of fear of poverty/destitution; We provide for them and for you (banning infanticide).
summaryEn: The root represents resource depletion and poverty (Imlaq), prohibiting pagan infant-killing practices driven by poverty fears and guaranteeing divine provision.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الفقر الطارئ المستأصل للأموال والخلو (إملاق)، حاظراً وأد الجاهلية للفتيات خشية الفقر والفاقة المادية (خشية إملاق).
rawText: تَمَلَّقَهُ 5 , (S, K,) and تَمَلَّقَ لَهُ, (S, Msb, K,) inf. n. تَمَلُّقٌ and تِمِلَّاقٌ, [like تِحِمَّالٌ and تِكِلَّامٌ, not تَمْلَاقٌ as in the CK,] He behaved in a loving, or an affectionate, and a blandishing, or coaxing, manner to him. (S, Msb, * K.) See a verse cited in art. رضو, conj. 5. مَلَقَهُ 1 He flayed him with a whip: like سَلَقَهُ. (TA in art. سلق.) مَلَقِيَّةٌ [A swiftly-running mare]. See عَبَّرَ بِهِ. مَلَّاقٌ Vehement in journeying, or in his pace; i. q. مَلَّاخٌ. (TA, voce مَلَّاخٌ.) مِمْلَقَةٌ A harrow: see مَلَّسَ.
